options.sentence: Many supporters expected Colombia to qualify after defeating DR Congo.
Many supporters expected Colombia qualified after defeating DR Congo.
Many supporters expected Colombia qualifying after defeating DR Congo.
Many supporters expected that Colombia to qualify after defeating DR Congo.
Many supporters expected Colombia was qualify after defeating DR Congo.

explanation.english: Certain verbs permit an object followed by an infinitive ('expect someone to do').
explanation.chinese: 某些动词后可接宾语加不定式，例如 'expect somebody to do'。

options.sentence: I don't think Turkey can still reach the Round of 32.
I think Turkey don't can still reach the Round of 32.
I don't think Turkey still can reaches the Round of 32.
I don't think that Turkey can still reaches the Round of 32.
I think not Turkey can still reach the Round of 32.

explanation.english: English often raises negation to the main verb: 'I don't think...'.
explanation.chinese: 英语常把否定提前到主句：'I don't think...'。

options.sentence: Several Mexican fans stopped celebrating only when the final whistle blew.
Several Mexican fans stopped to celebrate only when the final whistle blew.
Several Mexican fans stopped celebrate only when the final whistle blew.
Several Mexican fans stopping celebrating only when the final whistle blew.
Several Mexican fans stopped celebrated only when the final whistle blew.

explanation.english: 'Stop doing' means cease an activity, while 'stop to do' means pause in order to do something.
explanation.chinese: 'stop doing' 表示停止某事；'stop to do' 表示停下来去做另一件事。

options.sentence: The players have all adapted quickly to the new head-to-head rules.
The players all have adapted quickly to the new head-to-head rules.
The players have adapted all quickly to the new head-to-head rules.
The players has all adapted quickly to the new head-to-head rules.
The players have adapted quickly all to the new head-to-head rules.

explanation.english: Quantifiers such as 'all' may float, but some positions sound much more natural.
explanation.chinese: 像 all 这样的限定词可以浮动，但某些位置更自然。

options.sentence: A number of matches have already produced surprising results.
A number of matches has already produced surprising results.
The number of matches have already produced surprising results.
A number of matches is already produced surprising results.
A number of match have already produced surprising results.

explanation.english: 'A number of' takes a plural verb, whereas 'the number of' takes a singular verb.
explanation.chinese: 'A number of' 后接复数动词，而 'the number of' 后接单数动词。

options.sentence: It surprised many observers that Panama were eliminated so early.
It surprised many observers Panama were eliminated so early.
That Panama were eliminated so early surprised many observers it.
It surprising many observers that Panama were eliminated so early.
It surprised many observers that Panama was eliminated so earlyly.

explanation.english: Extraposition moves a heavy clause to the end and introduces 'it' as a dummy subject.
explanation.chinese: 外位结构把较长从句移到句末，用 it 作形式主语。

options.sentence: The teams selected for the Round of 32 will be announced on Saturday.
The teams selecting for the Round of 32 will be announced on Saturday.
The teams selected the Round of 32 will be announced on Saturday.
The teams which selected for the Round of 32 will be announced on Saturday.
The teams selected for the Round of 32 will announced on Saturday.

explanation.english: Past participles may reduce passive relative clauses.
explanation.chinese: 过去分词可以简化被动定语从句。

options.sentence: Be that as it may, Portugal still need at least a draw against Colombia.
Be that as it may be, Portugal still need at least a draw against Colombia.
Being that as it may, Portugal still need at least a draw against Colombia.
Be that as may, Portugal still need at least a draw against Colombia.
That as it may be, Portugal still need at least a draw against Colombia.

explanation.english: 'Be that as it may' is a fixed subjunctive expression meaning 'nevertheless'.
explanation.chinese: 'Be that as it may' 是固定虚拟语气表达，意为“尽管如此”。

options.sentence: Had England failed to defeat Croatia, qualification would now be far less certain.
Had England failed to defeat Croatia, qualification will now be far less certain.
Had England fail to defeat Croatia, qualification would now be far less certain.
Had England failed defeating Croatia, qualification would now be far less certain.
Had England failed to defeat Croatia, qualification would be now far less certain.

explanation.english: Mixed conditionals may combine a past unreal condition with a present consequence.
explanation.chinese: 混合虚拟条件句可以表示过去假设造成现在结果。

options.sentence: Harry Kane is believed to have scored twice against Croatia.
Harry Kane is believed scoring twice against Croatia.
Harry Kane is believed that he scored twice against Croatia.
Harry Kane is believed to scored twice against Croatia.
Harry Kane believed to have scored twice against Croatia.

explanation.english: Passive reporting verbs commonly take a perfect infinitive.
explanation.chinese: 被动报道动词后常接完成不定式。

options.sentence: England appeared to have underestimated Ghana's defensive organisation.
England appeared having underestimated Ghana's defensive organisation.
England appeared underestimated Ghana's defensive organisation.
England appeared to underestimated Ghana's defensive organisation.
England appeared that they underestimated Ghana's defensive organisation.

explanation.english: Perfect infinitives indicate an action completed before the main verb.
explanation.chinese: 完成不定式表示动作先于主要动词发生。

options.sentence: Only after Marcus Rashford scored in the eighty-fifth minute did England finally secure victory over Croatia.
Only after Marcus Rashford scored in the eighty-fifth minute England finally secured victory over Croatia.
Only after Marcus Rashford scored in the eighty-fifth minute did England finally secured victory over Croatia.
Only after Marcus Rashford scoring in the eighty-fifth minute did England finally secure victory over Croatia.
Only after Marcus Rashford scored in the eighty-fifth minute England did finally secure victory over Croatia.

explanation.english: Fronted 'only' expressions trigger inversion in the main clause.
explanation.chinese: 句首 only 短语会引起主句倒装。

options.sentence: It was Jude Bellingham who admitted that he had not deserved the award.
It was Jude Bellingham whom admitted that he had not deserved the award.
It was Jude Bellingham who admitted that he has not deserved the award.
It was Jude Bellingham admitted that he had not deserved the award.
It were Jude Bellingham who admitted that he had not deserved the award.

explanation.english: Cleft sentences use 'It was ... who/that ...' and maintain consistent tense usage.
explanation.chinese: 强调句使用 'It was ... who/that ...'，并保持时态一致。

options.sentence: Few England supporters expected Ghana to prevent Harry Kane from scoring.
Few England supporters expected Ghana prevented Harry Kane from scoring.
Few England supporters expected Ghana preventing Harry Kane from scoring.
Few England supporters expected that Ghana to prevent Harry Kane from scoring.
Few England supporters expected Ghana prevent Harry Kane from scoring.

explanation.english: Verbs such as 'expect' permit object-plus-infinitive constructions.
explanation.chinese: expect 等动词可以接宾语加不定式结构。
